title: Podman version difference between Ubuntu 18.04 and Ubuntu 20.x / 21.x
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**
Is the podman version suppose to be the same on Ubuntu 18.04 and higher?

If I look at [kubic download page](https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/) I see 

```
xUbuntu_18.04/ 14-May-2021 
xUbuntu_20.04/ | 21-May-2021 16:57 |
xUbuntu_20.10/ | 21-May-2021 16:47 |
xUbuntu_21.04/ | 21-May-2021 17:01 |
```
Is there a limitation of podman to only run on version 3.0.1 or has newer versions just not been built for 18.04?

comments.body: Please see https://podman.io/blogs/2021/03/02/podman-support-for-older-distros.html - the answer is that it should be technically possible, but maintenance for older distributions was becoming prohibitive for us. If the community wants to take over packaging for these distributions, we'd be happy to provide assistance, but we just don't have the manpower to continue doing it ourselves.
